metaTitle: Lost phone in Korea — recovery guide | Lost & Found Korea
metaDescription: Lost your iPhone or Android phone in Korea? Use Find My / Find My Device first, then let us recover and ship it home. English-speaking support.
heroBody: Phones are the most common item travelers lose in Korea — usually in taxis, on the subway, or at airport security. The good news: Korea's lost-and-found system handles them well, and Find My / Find My Device often pinpoints exactly where it is. Here's the right order to work through.

steps.title: Lock and locate it first
steps.body: Sign into iCloud (Find My) or Google (Find My Device) from any browser. Mark the phone as lost — this locks the screen and shows a custom message with your contact info. Note the last known location; that often tells us which venue to contact.

steps.title: Send us the location and circumstances
steps.body: Submit a free report with: where you last had it, the last known location from Find My, the make/model, and any case description. The more we know, the faster we can search the right venues in Korean.

steps.title: We call the venue in Korean
steps.body: Whether it's a Seoul taxi company, a subway lost-and-found, a hotel, or Incheon Airport's office — we make the calls in Korean, follow up daily, and confirm with you in your language.

steps.title: We pick it up and ship it home
steps.body: Once located, we collect the phone, package it carefully, and ship it via EMS or express courier to your address. Insurance and tracking included.

faqs.q: What if I disabled Find My before the trip?
faqs.a: We can still help — it just means searching by description and location instead. Provide the make, model, color, case, and last-seen venue, and we'll work the lost-and-found channels.

faqs.q: Can the phone be turned on / unlocked by the finder?
faqs.a: If you've enabled Find My / Find My Device's Lost Mode and screen lock, no. The phone is bricked to anyone but you. That's why locking it before reporting is the most important first step.

faqs.q: How long does recovery usually take?
faqs.a: Most phone cases resolve in 24–72 hours once we know which venue has it. Some take longer for items in transit (e.g., a phone left on a regional bus or train).

faqs.q: What about the eSIM and phone number?
faqs.a: Contact your carrier immediately to suspend the line and freeze any roaming charges. The phone hardware itself can still be recovered separately.
